title: salix gooddingii
intro: Salix gooddingii, also known as Goodding's willow, is a species of willow tree native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree with medicinal properties, primarily due to its bark and leaves containing salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The medicinal properties of Salix gooddingii have been used to treat fever, pain, and inflammation, as well as to aid in the treatment of colds and sore throats. Herbal preparations of Salix gooddingii can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, which can be used to relieve headaches, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations.
